disclaimer: This registry is for informational purposes only. Always consult official government sources and legal professionals for compliance requirements.

standards.title: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) - General Requirements
standards.summary: Employers must assess workplace hazards, select appropriate PPE, and ensure employees are trained on proper use and maintenance.
requirements: Select and provide appropriate PPE at no cost to employees
Provide training on PPE use, care, and disposal
Maintain and replace PPE as needed
standards.violations: Failure to provide PPE can result in fines up to $15,625 per violation
standards.keyPoints: PPE is the last line of defense; engineering controls must be considered first

standards.title: Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) - Control of Hazardous Energy
standards.summary: Establishes requirements to control hazardous energy during maintenance, servicing, and repair of machines and equipment.
requirements: Identify and label all energy sources
Train employees on the LOTO procedure
standards.violations: One of OSHA's most frequently cited standards with fines averaging $13,000
standards.keyPoints: All hazardous energy must be locked out and tagged out before service work

standards.title: General Requirements for All Machines
standards.summary: Requires all machines to be guarded to protect employees from hazardous parts.
requirements: Remove or guard all hazardous moving parts
Ensure guards prevent contact with hazards
Provide training on safe machine operation
standards.violations: Common citation for inadequate machine guarding
standards.keyPoints: Guards must be effective against the specific hazard and not create new hazards

standards.title: Hazard Communication (HazCom) Standard
standards.summary: Requires employers to provide information about hazardous chemicals through labels, Safety Data Sheets (SDS), and training.
requirements: Label all containers with hazard information
Provide Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for all chemicals
standards.violations: Frequently cited; fines for inadequate labeling or missing SDS
standards.keyPoints: Uses Globally Harmonized System (GHS) classification and labeling

standards.title: Fall Protection - Construction
standards.summary: Requires fall protection systems for all employees working at heights of 6 feet or more above a lower level.
requirements: Use fall arrest systems, safety nets, or warning lines
Ensure proper fit and connection of harnesses
standards.violations: Leading cause of worker deaths; severe penalties
standards.keyPoints: 6-foot rule applies; all systems must meet ANSI standards

UK.name: United Kingdom
UK.penaltyRange: Unlimited fines; Improvement Notices; Prohibition Notices; Prosecution

standards.title: Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974
standards.summary: The primary health and safety law in Great Britain. Employers must ensure the health, safety, and welfare of employees and others affected by their work.
requirements: Provide information, instruction, training, and supervision
Consult with employees on safety matters
standards.violations: Criminal liability; Crown Court can impose unlimited fines and imprisonment
standards.keyPoints: Covers employers, employees, self-employed, and manufacturers

standards.title: Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999
standards.summary: Specifies minimum requirements for managing health and safety. Requires risk assessments, safety policies, and emergency procedures.
requirements: Conduct risk assessment for all hazards
Implement control measures based on risk
standards.violations: Improvement Notices; Prohibition Notices; Prosecution with fines
standards.keyPoints: Foundation for all UK health and safety management

standards.title: Provision and Use of Work Equipment Regulations 1998
standards.summary: Requires employers to ensure work equipment is safe, properly maintained, and adequately guarded.
requirements: Ensure tools are suitable for the task
standards.violations: Enforcement by HSE; can result in prohibition of equipment use
standards.keyPoints: Covers all tools and equipment from hand tools to heavy machinery

standards.title: Personal Protective Equipment at Work Regulations 2016
standards.summary: Requires employers to provide appropriate PPE, ensure proper fit, and train employees on use.
requirements: Conduct assessment to determine PPE needs
Maintain and replace PPE as needed
standards.violations: Improvement Notices and fines for inadequate PPE provision
standards.keyPoints: PPE should be last line of defense after engineering controls

standards.title: Control of Substances Hazardous to Health (COSHH) Regulations 2002
standards.summary: Requires employers to assess and control exposure to hazardous substances.
standards.violations: Improvement Notices; fines for inadequate control measures
standards.keyPoints: Uses CHIPS labeling system for chemical hazard information

regions.name: Abu Dhabi
regions.authority: ADOSH (Abu Dhabi Occupational Safety and Health Center)

regions.name: Dubai
regions.authority: Dubai Municipality & Ministry of Human Resources and Emiratisation

standards.title: General Occupational Safety and Health Requirements
standards.summary: General requirement that employers provide a safe and healthy work environment for all employees.
requirements: Conduct hazard assessment for all work areas
Provide induction training for all workers
standards.violations: Administrative fines; closure of operations in severe cases
standards.keyPoints: Applies to all private and public sector employers

standards.title: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Requirements
standards.summary: Requires employers to assess and provide appropriate PPE for all workplace hazards.
requirements: Assess PPE requirements for each hazard
standards.violations: Fines for inadequate PPE provision; worker injuries attributed to lack of PPE
standards.keyPoints: All PPE must meet international standards (ISO, EN, ANSI)

standards.title: Machinery and Equipment Safety
standards.summary: Requires proper maintenance, guarding, and safe operation of all machinery and equipment.
requirements: Ensure machinery has proper guards and interlocks
Use only qualified contractors for repairs
Post safety warnings and operational procedures
standards.violations: Equipment seizure; fines for unsafe operations
standards.keyPoints: All machinery must comply with Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) standards

standards.title: Work at Height Safety Standards
standards.summary: Establishes requirements for safe work at heights (2 meters or above).
requirements: Train all workers on fall protection
Maintain rescue equipment and trained personnel
standards.violations: Severe penalties for fall accidents; shutdown of construction activities
standards.keyPoints: Work at height threshold is 2 meters in UAE (lower than international standards)

links.guidance: Fall Protection Guidelines available from ADOSH

standards.title: Chemical Hazard Control and Management
standards.summary: Requires identification, assessment, and control of chemical hazards in the workplace.
standards.violations: Fines for improper chemical handling; environmental penalties
standards.keyPoints: GHS labeling system is recommended; local authorities may have specific requirements

LUXEMBOURG.name: Luxembourg
LUXEMBOURG.authority: ADEM (Agence pour le développement de l'emploi - Employment and Occupational Health Agency)
LUXEMBOURG.enforcementAgency: Inspection du Travail et des Mines (Labor and Mines Inspection)
LUXEMBOURG.penaltyRange: EUR 500 - EUR 100,000 depending on violation severity
LUXEMBOURG.euDirectives: Implements EU-wide Occupational Safety and Health Directives

standards.title: General Obligation to Protect Workers' Health and Safety
standards.summary: Employers must take measures to protect the health and safety of workers, in accordance with EU directives and national law.
requirements: Conduct risk assessment for all workplaces
standards.violations: Administrative fines; criminal liability for serious breaches
standards.keyPoints: Based on EU Framework Directive 89/391/EEC; goes beyond minimum requirements

links.guidance: ADEM guidance documents available on website

standards.title: Use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
standards.summary: Implements EU Directive 89/656/EEC on PPE use requirements.
requirements: Assess need for PPE based on risk assessment
Provide PPE at no cost to workers
standards.violations: Fines for failure to provide appropriate PPE; worker compensation claims
standards.keyPoints: All PPE must bear CE mark and meet EN or ISO standards

standards.title: Machinery Safety and Use of Work Equipment
standards.summary: Implements EU Directive 2006/42/EC on machinery safety and equipment use.
requirements: Install proper guards and emergency stops
standards.violations: Equipment seizure; fines for unsafe machinery
standards.keyPoints: Covers both new and used machinery; high standards for safety

standards.title: Chemical Substances and Hazardous Substances Labeling
standards.summary: Implements EU REACH Regulation and CLP Regulation on chemical safety and labeling.
requirements: Register chemicals with ECHA if applicable
Label containers with hazard pictograms and warnings
standards.violations: Significant fines for non-compliance with REACH/CLP; product seizure
standards.keyPoints: EU-wide standards ensure consistency; strict enforcement

standards.title: Work at Height and Fall Protection
standards.summary: Implements EU Temporary or Mobile Work Sites Directive on fall protection.
requirements: Assess fall hazards (2 meters or more)
standards.violations: Severe fines; shutdown of work activities if violations present
standards.keyPoints: EU standards require 2-meter threshold; high safety requirements

links.guidance: EU Directive 92/57/EEC on Temporary Work Sites
